{{Short description="Family of elementary particles including the electron and neutrino}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Lepton
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Elementary particle]], [[Fermion]], [[Electron]], [[Neutrino]], [[Standard Model]]
| composition = [[Elementary particle]]
| statistics = [[Fermion]]
| types = Electron, muon, tau (+ neutrinos)
| spin = 1/2
}}

In [[particle physics]], a '''lepton''' is an [[elementary particle]] of half-integer [[spin (physics)|spin]] ([[fermion]]) that does not undergo [[strong interaction]]s. The name "lepton" comes from the Greek λεπτός (''leptos''), meaning "small" or "thin."

There are six types of leptons, arranged in three generations, plus their [[antiparticle]]s:
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Generation !! Charged lepton !! Neutral lepton (neutrino) !! Charge
|-
| 1st || [[Electron]] (e⁻) || νₑ (electron neutrino) || −1
|-
| 2nd || [[Muon]] (μ⁻) || ν_μ (muon neutrino) || −1
|-
| 3rd || [[Tau (particle)|Tau]] (τ⁻) || ν_τ (tau neutrino) || −1
|}

== Properties ==
* Spin 1/2 (fermions)
* Subject to [[weak interaction]] and (if charged) [[electromagnetism]]
* Do NOT experience the [[strong interaction]]
* Each charged lepton has an associated [[neutrino]] (neutral lepton)
* [[Lepton number]] is conserved in the Standard Model (separately for each generation)

== Masses ==
* [[Electron]]: 0.511 MeV/c² (lightest charged lepton, stable)
* [[Muon]]: 105.7 MeV/c² (mean lifetime ~2.2 μs)
* [[Tau (particle)|Tau]]: 1,777 MeV/c² (mean lifetime ~2.9×10⁻¹³ s)
* Neutrinos: masses < 2 eV (electron neutrino), exact values unknown

== Conservation of lepton number ==
Each lepton generation has a conserved quantum number <math>L_e, L_\mu, L_\tau</math>. For example:
* Electron and νₑ have <math>L_e = +1</math>
* Positron and anti-νₑ have <math>L_e = -1</math>

[[Neutrino oscillation]]s violate individual lepton number conservation, implying physics beyond the Standard Model.

== Role in nature ==
* [[Electron]]s form [[atom]]s and determine chemical properties
* [[Muon]]s are produced in cosmic ray interactions and used in [[muon tomography]]
* [[Tau (particle)|Tau]] leptons are produced in high-energy particle collisions
* [[Neutrino]]s are the most abundant massive particles in the universe

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Electron]]
* [[Elementary particle]]
* [[Fermion]]
* [[Muon]]
* [[Neutrino]]
* [[Standard Model]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Leptons| ]]
[[Category:Elementary particles]]
[[Category:Particle physics]]
